---
title: "Python for Financial Analysis"
subtitle: "An R User's Guide to pandas and Beyond"
---

## Welcome

This tutorial teaches Python for financial and workforce analysis, designed specifically for analysts who already know R. Using real University of Wisconsin-Madison salary data spanning 2021-2025, you'll learn to translate your R/tidyverse skills to Python/pandas while exploring practical financial analysis concepts.

## Who This Is For

This tutorial assumes you:

- **Know R well** - You're comfortable with dplyr, tidyr, and ggplot2
- **Understand data analysis** - You've worked with real datasets and know about joins, aggregations, and data cleaning
- **Have domain context** - Familiarity with higher education or public sector data is helpful but not required

You'll find collapsible "In R" callouts throughout that show equivalent R code for each Python concept.

## What You'll Learn

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
### Python Fundamentals
- Setting up PyCharm and virtual environments
- Python syntax differences from R
- pandas DataFrames and Series
- Data selection, filtering, and transformation
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Financial Analysis
- Inflation adjustment with CPI data
- Compensation analysis and peer comparisons
- Workforce analytics (headcount, FTE, turnover)
- Tracking individuals over time
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Data Skills
- Working with APIs (FRED economic data)
- Web scraping job descriptions
- String operations and data cleaning
- Date/time manipulation
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Visualization & Production
- Interactive charts with Plotly
- Quarto dashboards
- Building reusable data pipelines
- Capstone analysis project
:::

:::

## The Data

We use publicly available salary records from UW-Madison, including:

- **8 snapshots** from November 2021 to September 2025
- **~25,000+ employee records** per snapshot
- **System transition** from UFAS (legacy) to Workday (2025)
- **Rich metadata** including job classifications, divisions, and departments

This data presents real-world challenges: inconsistent formatting, organizational restructuring, and multiple data sources to merge.

## Lesson Structure

Each lesson includes:

1. **Learning objectives** - What you'll be able to do
2. **Concept explanations** - With R comparisons in collapsible callouts
3. **Worked examples** - Using the salary data
4. **Exercises** - Practice problems with solutions

## Getting Started

Ready to begin? Start with [Lesson 1: Setting Up Your Python Environment](lessons/01-setup.qmd).

::: {.callout-note}
## Prerequisites
Before starting, you should have:

- A computer with admin rights to install software
- Basic familiarity with command line/terminal
- The tutorial data files (included in this repository)
:::

## Tutorial Outline

### Part 1: Foundations
1. [Setting Up Your Python Environment](lessons/01-setup.qmd)
2. [Python Syntax for R Users](lessons/02-syntax.qmd)
3. [Introduction to pandas DataFrames](lessons/03-pandas-intro.qmd)
4. [Data Selection and Filtering](lessons/04-selection-filtering.qmd)

### Part 2: Data Transformation
5. [Reshaping and Aggregation](lessons/05-reshaping-aggregation.qmd)
6. [Joining and Merging Data](lessons/06-joining-merging.qmd)
7. [Working with Dates and Time Series](lessons/07-dates-timeseries.qmd)
8. [String Operations](lessons/08-string-operations.qmd)

### Part 3: Financial Analysis
9. [Inflation Adjustment and Real vs Nominal Values](lessons/09-inflation-adjustment.qmd)
10. [Compensation Analysis and Distributions](lessons/10-compensation-analysis.qmd)
11. [Workforce Analytics: Headcount and FTE](lessons/11-workforce-analytics.qmd)
12. [Tracking Individuals Over Time](lessons/12-tracking-individuals.qmd)

### Part 4: Visualization
13. [Static Visualization with Plotly](lessons/13-plotly-basics.qmd)
14. [Interactive Dashboards in Quarto](lessons/14-interactive-dashboards.qmd)

### Part 5: Data Acquisition
15. [Working with APIs](lessons/15-working-with-apis.qmd)
16. [Web Scraping Basics](lessons/16-web-scraping.qmd)

### Part 6: Production Patterns
17. [Building Reusable Data Pipelines](lessons/17-reusable-pipelines.qmd)
18. [Capstone: Complete Analysis Report](lessons/18-capstone.qmd)

### Part 7: Advanced Financial Analysis
19. [Inequality Metrics](lessons/19-inequality-metrics.qmd)
20. [Salary Compression Analysis](lessons/20-salary-compression.qmd)
21. [Statistical Testing for Equity](lessons/21-statistical-testing.qmd)
22. [Forecasting and Trend Modeling](lessons/22-forecasting.qmd)
23. [Cost Modeling and Budget Impact](lessons/23-cost-modeling.qmd)
24. [Markov Chain Workforce Modeling](lessons/24-markov-workforce.qmd)
25. [Peer Institution Comparison](lessons/25-peer-comparison.qmd)
